description: Audience taxonomy. Every piece of canonical content — rule, framework, doc, skill, hook — declares its primary audience. Three top-level categories; sub-categories let agents pick the right tone, depth, and tool surface.

human.description: Carbon-based developers reading docs or running scripts directly. Default for top-level docs.

human/contributor.description: Someone building or modifying wave-foundation itself. Read every doc carefully.

human/consumer.description: Engineer adopting wave-foundation in their own project via consume.sh / the plugin.

human/operator.description: SRE / oncall responding to alerts; runs scripts/* + reads runbooks.
human/operator.tone: <60s to action; symptom → first command → diagnosis

agent.description: AI agents (Claude, Codex, Cursor, Gemini, Grok, Copilot) reading instructions and making code edits.

agent/main.description: The primary session-level agent. Reads AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md / GEMINI.md at session start.

agent/subagent.description: Dispatched subagent (Task tool, agent-context-injector). Cold start; needs explicit context.
agent/subagent.tone: self-contained brief: goal + constraints + deliverable + word cap

agent/hook.description: PreToolUse / PostToolUse / Stop hooks. Headless; no conversational context.

user.description: End users of wave-built apps. Never reads canonical content directly; this taxonomy entry covers content shown TO them (error messages, status pages, voice output).

user/end.description: Authenticated paying user of a WAVE app.
user/end.tone: plain language; no internal jargon; honest about scope

user/anonymous.description: Pre-auth visitor; marketing / docs site readers.
user/anonymous.tone: approachable; assumes no prior context; SEO-aware

rules.multi_audience_content: Pick the PRIMARY audience. Note secondary in the doc's header (e.g. '> Audience: human/operator (primary), agent/main (secondary)'). Don't try to serve both with one tone.
